features.name: Project Numbering (PR-TOKEN-NNNN)
features.description: Top-layer Project records receive a unique human-readable number in the format PR-{ORG_TOKEN}-{NNNN}, e.g. PR-KC007-0001. Sequence is per-org, MAX+1, and independent from the FL- (Case) sequence so the two layers never collide.
risks: No database-level unique constraint confirmed in code; relies on MAX+1 from lexical DESC order. Concurrent inserts could theoretically produce duplicate numbers if two requests race before either writes.

features.name: Multi-tenant Org Scoping
features.description: Every project read, write, patch, delete, and sub-resource query is filtered by the caller's org_id derived from the JWT via require_org. Service-role client is used (bypassing RLS), so org_id is always applied explicitly in every query.
risks: Service-role client bypasses Supabase RLS — if org_id filter is accidentally omitted in any branch, data leaks cross-tenant.

features.name: Project-Case-Inquiry Chain Resolution
features.description: A Project owns no inquiries, appointments, cost-estimates, invoices, or calls directly. All sub-resources are resolved via the chain: Project → cases (cases.project_id) → inquiries (inquiries.case_id) → calls/appointments/KVAs/invoices. De-duplication by id is applied when the same row is reachable via both case_id and inquiry_id.
risks: Large orgs with many cases/inquiries could trigger many sequential or parallel DB calls. fetch_all_rows pages past the 1000-row PostgREST cap.

features.name: Auto-Create Case on Appointment Confirm
features.description: When an appointment is confirmed (either manually via POST /appointments/{id}/confirm or by the post-call agent), the system optionally auto-creates a Case and links it to the appointment. Controlled by agent_configs.projects_enabled + projects_level. Best-effort — never blocks the confirmation.
risks: Config gate uses legacy flag names (projects_enabled / projects_level) that now control case auto-creation — the flag name mismatch is a readability/maintenance risk.
Best-effort pattern: failures are logged but silently ignored — case creation failures are not surfaced to the user.

features.name: Auto-Assign Inquiry to Case (Embedding-Based Grouping)
features.description: Every new inquiry (from inbound call ingest or agent tool) is automatically filed into a Case. The system embeds the inquiry's content (summary + transcript), compares it against up to 8 open cases for the same customer using cosine similarity, and attaches to the best match if similarity >= 0.70. Otherwise a new Case is created. Best-effort — never breaks inquiry creation.
risks: No database-level uniqueness constraint on cases.number confirmed.
AI embedding failure silently falls back to creating a new case — may create more cases than expected.
Attach threshold (0.70) is hardcoded; no per-org configuration.

features.name: Project CRUD with Customer IDOR Protection
features.description: On Project create and update, if a customer_id is supplied, it is validated via validate_fk_in_org to confirm the customer belongs to the same org. Cross-tenant IDOR is blocked at the application layer (not at RLS, since the service-role client bypasses it).
risks: validate_fk_in_org is a no-op when fk_id is falsy — clearing customer_id is always allowed without validation, which is the intended behavior but should be noted.

features.name: Project Team via Case-Level case_employees
features.description: Projects have no project_employees table. The project team is the UNION of its member cases' case_employees rows, de-duplicated by employee_id (earliest added_at wins). Adding an employee to a project fans out the insert to EVERY member case. Removing does the same.
risks: Adding an employee to a project with zero member cases returns HTTP 400 — the error is only surfaced when no cases exist.
Fan-out insert to all cases uses try/except to swallow unique-constraint violations (employee already in case). If a non-unique-constraint error occurs it is also silently ignored.

features.name: Soft Delete via Archive
features.description: DELETE /api/projects/{id} does not physically delete the record. It sets status = 'archived' and updates updated_at. The project remains in the database and is still returned by GET /api/projects unless filtered by status.
risks: Users who issue DELETE expecting hard-delete may be confused by the project reappearing in an 'all status' query.

features.name: Workspace Tabs (Project Detail View)
features.description: The ProjectWorkspacePage renders 10 tabs: Übersicht, Fälle, Anrufe, Anfragen, Termine, Kostenvoranschläge, Rechnungen, Team, Dokumente, Notizen. Tab state is managed client-side (URL param ?tab=). Each tab fetches its own endpoint. Case status labels in the Fälle tab differ from the project status labels intentionally (Offen/In Arbeit/Fertig vs In Planung/In Bearbeitung/Abgeschlossen).
risks: InquiriesTab NewInquiryModal posts project_id directly to /api/inquiries but the inquiries table has no project_id column post-migration-0073 — this may be a dead/stale field in the request payload. UNVERIFIED OBSERVATION.

features.name: Project-from-Case Form Context
features.description: When ProjectFormPage is launched from a Case (via ?customer_id=&case_id=&case_number= URL params), the customer field is pre-filled and locked (disabled), the customer address is auto-copied into the project address fields, and on save the originating Case is automatically linked to the new Project via POST /api/projects/{id}/cases.
risks: The German address is parsed from a free-text raw blob by a client-side regex (5-digit PLZ detection); edge cases in address formatting may produce incorrect street/PLZ/city split.

rules.name: Project Number Generation (PR-TOKEN-NNNN)
rules.description: When a Project is created, it is assigned a unique number of the form PR-{ORG_TOKEN}-{NNNN} (e.g. PR-KC007-0001). The org token is derived from organizations.case_prefix (or computed from org name/slug/code and persisted on first use). The sequence number is MAX+1 over existing PR-{TOKEN}-NNNN numbers for this org in the projects table, queried in descending lexical order and parsed from the trailing digits. Deleted records never re-issue their number (MAX rather than COUNT).
rules.purpose: Gives staff a human-readable, org-namespaced project reference that is stable even after deletions, and does not collide with the FL- (Case) sequence.
preconditions: Authenticated user with a valid org_id
actions: Call get_org_token to read/derive/persist organizations.case_prefix
Call _max_seq_for_token over projects table with prefix PR-{TOKEN}-
Increment by 1 and zero-pad to 4 digits
systemEffects: May write organizations.case_prefix on first use if not yet set
failureConditions: If organizations row not found, falls back to token 'X00'
rules.evidence: gen_project_number: prefix = f'PR-{get_org_token(client, org_id)}-'; seq = _max_seq_for_token(client, 'projects', org_id, prefix) + 1; return f'{prefix}{seq:04d}'

rules.name: Org Token Derivation and Persistence
rules.description: The org token used in project and case numbers is read from organizations.case_prefix. If not yet set, it is derived from the org's name/slug/code (e.g. initials + numeric suffix), disambiguated against sibling orgs that already have the same token, and then written back to organizations.case_prefix. Falls back to the legacy get_org_code path if the case_prefix column is absent.
rules.purpose: Ensures each org has a stable, globally unique token that makes record numbers readable and namespaced.
rules.trigger: Any call to gen_project_number or gen_case_number
validations: Checks for clash (another org with same case_prefix) and appends org code suffix if needed
actions: Read organizations.case_prefix
If absent: derive token from name/slug/code, check for clashes, write back
systemEffects: Writes organizations.case_prefix (best-effort, failure doesn't break numbering)
failureConditions: If organizations row not found: returns 'X00'
If write fails: continues with derived token but does not persist
rules.evidence: get_org_token reads organizations.case_prefix; if not set derives + persists with clash check. Falls back to get_org_code if column exception.

rules.name: Project Create — Customer IDOR Validation
rules.description: On POST /api/projects, if customer_id is provided in the payload, the backend calls validate_fk_in_org to confirm the customer record exists in the projects org. If the customer belongs to a different org, HTTP 422 is raised. A null/empty customer_id bypasses the check (optional field).
rules.purpose: Prevents cross-tenant data linkage (IDOR): an attacker cannot attach another org's customer to their own project using a guessed UUID.
validations: customers row with id=customer_id AND org_id=caller_org must exist
actions: Insert project row with validated customer_id
failureConditions: HTTP 422 if customer_id belongs to a different org ('Kunde gehört nicht zu dieser Organisation.')
rules.evidence: validate_fk_in_org(client, table='customers', fk_id=payload.customer_id, org_id=org_id, label='Kunde') called before insert

rules.name: Project Create — Default Status Planning
rules.description: When creating a project, if status is not specified in the payload, it defaults to 'planning'. The valid status set is {planning, active, completed, archived}.
rules.purpose: New projects start in a draft/planning state, requiring explicit promotion to active by staff.
validations: Status must be in {'planning','active','completed','archived'} if provided on PATCH
actions: Set status = payload.status or 'planning' on insert
outputs: Project row with status = 'planning' if not specified
failureConditions: PATCH with invalid status raises HTTP 422 ('Invalid status')
rules.evidence: 'status': payload.status or 'planning' in _create; _STATUSES = {'planning', 'active', 'completed', 'archived'}; PATCH validates status in _STATUSES

rules.name: Notes Timestamp on Write
rules.description: Whenever internal_notes is written (on create or PATCH), notes_updated_at is set to the current UTC timestamp. This allows the UI to display 'last saved X minutes ago' and present a session-level edit history (client-side only, not persisted).
rules.purpose: Audit trail for internal note changes; enables UI to show recency of notes.
rules.trigger: POST /api/projects (if internal_notes non-empty), PATCH /api/projects/{id} (if internal_notes in payload)
preconditions: internal_notes is present and non-null in the payload
actions: Set notes_updated_at = UTC now()
rules.evidence: if payload.internal_notes: row['notes_updated_at'] = _now() in _create; if 'internal_notes' in fields: fields['notes_updated_at'] = _now() in _patch

rules.name: Soft Delete — Archive on DELETE
rules.description: DELETE /api/projects/{id} does NOT physically remove the project. It sets status = 'archived' and updated_at = now(). The row persists in the database and is returned by list/detail unless filtered by status.
rules.purpose: Preserves project history and linked financial/case data. Archived projects can be retrieved or restored.
actions: UPDATE projects SET status='archived', updated_at=now() WHERE org_id=? AND id=?
failureConditions: HTTP 404 if project not found or not in org
rules.evidence: DELETE handler: client.table('projects').update({'status': 'archived', 'updated_at': _now()}).eq('org_id',...).eq('id',...); returns {'success': True, 'status': 'archived'}

rules.name: Project List — Full Pagination (No Silent Truncation)
rules.description: GET /api/projects fetches ALL projects for the org using fetch_all_rows (pages past the PostgREST 1000-row cap). Sub-resource counts (cases, inquiries, appointments, etc.) are resolved in parallel for the full set. Search filtering (title or customer_name) is applied in-process after fetching all rows.
rules.purpose: Ensures that large orgs do not silently miss projects due to PostgREST's implicit row limit.
actions: Page all projects via fetch_all_rows
Batch-fetch cases, inquiries, appointments, KVAs, invoices, employees in parallel
Roll up counts per project
Apply search filter in-process
outputs: List of projects with stats, customer_name, progress, actual_budget, open_amount
rules.evidence: Comment: 'Page past the 1000-row cap: the list is filtered/searched client-side, so it must contain EVERY project'; projects = fetch_all_rows(_projects_query)

rules.name: Project Status Lifecycle (four states)
rules.description: A project has four statuses: planning, active, completed, archived. Status can be changed via PATCH /api/projects/{id} to any valid value; any invalid value raises HTTP 422. The UI additionally allows inline status change from a dropdown in the TopBar. The frontend maps statuses to German labels: planning=In Planung, active=In Bearbeitung, completed=Abgeschlossen, archived=Archiviert.
rules.purpose: Models the workflow stages of a project from inception to closure.
rules.trigger: PATCH /api/projects/{id} with status field; UI status dropdown
actions: UPDATE projects SET status=?, updated_at=now()
failureConditions: HTTP 422 if status not in valid set
HTTP 404 if project not found
rules.evidence: _STATUSES = {'planning', 'active', 'completed', 'archived'}; if 'status' in fields and fields['status'] not in _STATUSES: raise HTTPException(status_code=422, detail='Invalid status')

rules.name: Case-to-Project Link — Same Customer Validation
rules.description: POST /api/projects/{project_id}/cases links a Case to a Project by setting cases.project_id. Before linking, the backend verifies: (a) the case exists in the org; (b) if both the project and the case have a customer_id, they must be the same customer. A case from a different customer cannot be attached.
rules.purpose: A Project bundles multiple Cases for a single customer. Cross-customer case attachment would corrupt the project's data scope.
validations: If project.customer_id and case.customer_id: they must be equal
actions: UPDATE cases SET project_id=project_id, updated_at=now() WHERE org_id=? AND id=case_id
failureConditions: HTTP 404 if project not found
HTTP 404 if case not found
HTTP 422 if case belongs to a different customer ('Dieser Fall gehört zu einem anderen Kunden — ein Projekt bündelt nur Fälle desselben Kunden.')
rules.evidence: if proj.get('customer_id') and case.get('customer_id') and proj['customer_id'] != case['customer_id']: return 'wrong_customer' → HTTP 422

rules.name: Case-from-Project Unlink (Null project_id)
rules.description: DELETE /api/projects/{project_id}/cases/{case_id} clears cases.project_id to NULL. The operation only succeeds if the case is currently linked to THIS project (cases.project_id = project_id guard). If the case is linked to a different project or is unlinked, HTTP 404 is returned.
rules.purpose: Allows staff to re-assign or remove cases from a project without deleting either record.
preconditions: Case is linked to this project
validations: cases.project_id == project_id (atomically checked in UPDATE WHERE clause)
actions: UPDATE cases SET project_id=NULL, updated_at=now() WHERE org_id=? AND id=? AND project_id=?
failureConditions: HTTP 404 if project not found
HTTP 404 if case not linked to this project ('Fall ist diesem Projekt nicht zugeordnet')
rules.evidence: UPDATE cases SET project_id=None WHERE org_id=? AND id=case_id AND project_id=project_id; returns 'ok' if res.data else 'not_linked'

rules.name: Team Fan-Out — Add Employee to All Member Cases
rules.description: POST /api/projects/{id}/employees adds an employee to a project's team by inserting a case_employees row for EVERY member case (cases where cases.project_id = project_id). Unique constraint violations (employee already in case) are silently swallowed. If the project has no member cases, HTTP 400 is returned.
rules.purpose: There is no project_employees table; team is tracked at the case level. Fan-out ensures the employee appears in the project's aggregated team (UNION over cases).
preconditions: Employee exists in org with deleted=False
Project has at least one member case
validations: Employee must exist in org and not be deleted (validate via employees.eq('org_id').eq('id').eq('deleted', False))
Project must have at least one case
actions: For each case_id in project's cases: INSERT INTO case_employees (case_id, employee_id) (ignore unique violations)
systemEffects: Inserts rows into case_employees for every member case
failureConditions: HTTP 404 if project not found
HTTP 404 if employee not found or deleted ('Mitarbeiter nicht gefunden')
HTTP 400 if project has no member cases
rules.evidence: for cid in case_ids: try: client.table('case_employees').insert({'case_id': cid, 'employee_id': payload.employee_id}).execute() except Exception: pass  # already assigned

rules.name: Team Fan-Out — Remove Employee from All Member Cases
rules.description: DELETE /api/projects/{id}/employees/{employee_id} removes the employee from EVERY member case (DELETE from case_employees WHERE case_id IN (project's case_ids) AND employee_id=?). This mirrors the fan-out add in PROJ-011.
rules.purpose: Mirrors the add fan-out: removing a project-level team member must clear them from all underlying case teams.
actions: DELETE FROM case_employees WHERE case_id IN (project case_ids) AND employee_id=?
systemEffects: Removes case_employees rows for all member cases
failureConditions: HTTP 404 if project not found
rules.evidence: client.table('case_employees').delete().in_('case_id', case_ids).eq('employee_id', employee_id).execute()

rules.name: Project Team — Earliest added_at Wins De-duplication
rules.description: When reading the project team (GET /api/projects/{id}/employees), the system unions case_employees rows across all member cases. If the same employee_id appears in multiple cases, a single entry is returned using the earliest added_at timestamp across all the case memberships.
rules.purpose: Presents a deduplicated project team where the employee's seniority is measured from their first case assignment.
actions: Fetch case_employees for all member cases
De-dup by employee_id: keep earliest added_at
Fetch employee display_name, role, calendar_color
Count appointments handled per employee across the project
outputs: List of {id, name, role, color, appointments_handled, added_at}
failureConditions: HTTP 404 if project not found
rules.evidence: first_added: dict[str, str] = {}; for e in pe: eid = e['employee_id']; if eid not in first_added or (e.get('added_at') or '') < (first_added[eid] or ''): first_added[eid] = e.get('added_at')

rules.name: Invoice Overdue Computed Status
rules.description: In GET /api/projects/{id}/invoices, the backend adds a computed 'overdue' status: if an invoice's status == 'sent' AND due_date < today (Berlin date), the status field is mutated to 'overdue' in the response. This is a presentation-layer computation — the underlying DB row remains 'sent'.
rules.purpose: Surfaces overdue payment obligations without a separate DB status value.
actions: For each invoice: if status == 'sent' and due_date < today: set status = 'overdue' in response
outputs: Invoice rows with possibly-mutated status field
rules.evidence: if r.get('status') == 'sent' and r.get('due_date') and str(r['due_date']) < today: r['status'] = 'overdue'

rules.name: Document Upload — Stored Against Customer (Not Case)
rules.description: POST /api/projects/{id}/documents uploads a file to the Supabase 'customer-files' bucket at path {org_id}/projects/{project_id}/{uuid}_{filename}. The resulting documents row is inserted with customer_id = project.customer_id but NO case_id (projects have no case_id FK on the documents table). The document is re-discovered via the project documents endpoint through the customer fan-in (customer-wide docs included in GET /api/projects/{id}/documents).
rules.purpose: Persists project-level documents in a way that survives case reorganization, since the project has no direct case_id on documents.
actions: Upload file to Supabase Storage bucket 'customer-files' at {org_id}/projects/{project_id}/{uuid}_{filename}
INSERT INTO documents (org_id, customer_id, name, path, category, mime_type, size_bytes, is_image)
systemEffects: documents row inserted with customer_id = project.customer_id, no case_id
outputs: Document row with signed URL (1-hour expiry)
failureConditions: HTTP 413 if file > 10MB
HTTP 404 if project not found
rules.evidence: row = client.table('documents').insert({'org_id': user.org_id, 'customer_id': p.get('customer_id'), ...}); comment: 'A top-layer project has no case_id column on documents; file it against the customer'

rules.name: Document List — Customer-Wide Fan-In
rules.description: GET /api/projects/{id}/documents returns documents via two paths: (1) documents linked to the project's cases/inquiries (via _action_rows); (2) documents linked to the project's customer (customer_id = project.customer_id, no case/inquiry). Both sets are merged and de-duplicated by id. All documents include a 1-hour signed URL from Supabase Storage.
rules.purpose: Makes customer-level documents (e.g. photos uploaded directly to a customer) visible within the project context, even when they have no case_id.
actions: Fetch documents via case_ids and inquiry_ids (_action_rows)
Fetch customer-wide documents if project.customer_id is set
De-dup by id, sort by uploaded_at desc
Generate signed URLs for each document
outputs: List of document rows with url field
failureConditions: HTTP 404 if project not found
rules.evidence: customer-wide docs: if p.get('customer_id'): for d in client.table('documents').select('*').eq('org_id',...).eq('customer_id', p['customer_id']).execute().data or []: if d['id'] not in seen: rows.append(d)

rules.name: Auto-Create Case on Appointment Confirm (Agent Config Gate)
rules.description: When an appointment is confirmed (POST /api/appointments/{id}/confirm or post-call auto-confirm), the system calls maybe_create_case_for_appointment. It reads agent_configs.projects_enabled and projects_level for the org. If projects_enabled is False or level <= 1: no-op. If level == 2: create a case with status 'planning'. If level >= 3: create a case with status 'active'. The case is linked to the appointment via appointments.case_id. The function is best-effort and never raises (failures logged only).
rules.purpose: Automates case creation from appointment confirmation as a back-office workflow, configurable per org.
actions: Read agent_configs.projects_enabled and projects_level
INSERT INTO cases (org_id, customer_id, number, title, description, status, created_by)
UPDATE appointments SET case_id = new_case_id WHERE org_id=? AND id=?
systemEffects: Case created in cases table with status 'planning' (level=2) or 'active' (level>=3)
outputs: Created case row, or None if gated off
failureConditions: Any exception: logged as WARNING, function returns None (never raises)
rules.evidence: if not row.get('projects_enabled'): return None; level = int(row.get('projects_level') or 2); if level <= 1: return None; status='active' if level >= 3 else 'planning'

rules.name: Case Number Generation (FL-TOKEN-NNNN)
rules.description: Case (Fall) numbers use the format FL-{ORG_TOKEN}-{NNNN}, e.g. FL-KC007-0001. This is a completely separate sequence from Project numbers (PR-). Both use _max_seq_for_token but over different tables (cases vs projects) and with different prefixes (FL- vs PR-). Numbers are never reused after deletion (MAX+1).
rules.purpose: Distinguishes the Case layer (FL-) from the Project layer (PR-) in human-readable record identifiers.
rules.trigger: Case creation (auto from inquiry, auto from appointment confirm, or direct INSERT)
actions: get_org_token to resolve/derive org token
_max_seq_for_token over cases table with FL- prefix
Return FL-{TOKEN}-{NNNN:04d}
rules.evidence: def gen_case_number: prefix = f'FL-{get_org_token(client, org_id)}-'; seq = _max_seq_for_token(client, 'cases', org_id, prefix) + 1; return f'{prefix}{seq:04d}'

rules.name: Auto-Assign New Inquiry to Case (Embedding Grouper)
rules.description: On every new inquiry creation (inbound call ingest via create_inquiry_from_call or agent tool via create_inquiry), the function safe_auto_assign is called. It: (1) no-ops if the inquiry already has a case_id; (2) fetches up to 8 open cases (status in planning/active) for the same customer ordered by created_at desc; (3) if AI is within cap, embeds the inquiry signal (subject+summary+notes+transcript) and each case's signal (title+description+member inquiry summaries), computes cosine similarity, and attaches to the best case if similarity >= 0.70; (4) on any failure (embed error, AI cap exceeded, no open cases, similarity below threshold) creates a new case.
rules.purpose: Automatically groups related inquiries under the same Case, reducing staff effort to manually organize inbound calls.
rules.trigger: Inquiry creation from call ingest (inquiries.py:create_inquiry_from_call) or agent tool (inquiries.py:create_inquiry)
inputs: inquiry dict (id, customer_id, subject, title, notes)
actions: Fetch up to _MAX_OPEN_CASES (8) open cases for customer
If AI within cap: embed inquiry signal and case signals using text-embedding-3-small
Compute cosine similarity between inquiry and each case
If best_sim >= _ATTACH_SIM (0.70): UPDATE inquiries SET case_id, case_source='ai', case_confidence, case_reason

Else: INSERT INTO cases (org_id, customer_id, number, title, description='Automatisch aus neuer Anfrage erstellt.', status='active'), then UPDATE inquiries SET case_id, case_source='ai', case_confidence=1.0, case_reason='automatisch: neuer Fall'
systemEffects: inquiries.case_id set (either to existing or new case)
inquiries.case_confidence set to similarity score or 1.0
New case row may be created in cases table
outputs: Case dict, or None if inquiry already had case_id
failureConditions: Any exception: logged as WARNING, returns None (safe_auto_assign wrapper never raises)
rules.evidence: _ATTACH_SIM = 0.70; _MAX_OPEN_CASES = 8; _MEMBER_SAMPLE = 6; if best_sim >= _ATTACH_SIM: return _attach(...); return _create_case_for_inquiry(...)

rules.name: Inquiry-to-Case Grouping — Audit Trail Columns
rules.description: When an inquiry is attached to a case (either by AI similarity match or new-case creation), three columns are written on the inquiries row: case_source ('ai'), case_confidence (similarity score 0-1, or 1.0 for auto-create), and case_reason (German text explaining the decision). This audit trail is shared with the manual grouping workflow.
rules.purpose: Provides full auditability of how each inquiry was assigned to its case, distinguishing AI auto-assignment from manual moves.
rules.trigger: AI attach or create in projects_auto.auto_assign_inquiry_to_case
actions: UPDATE inquiries SET case_id, case_source='ai', case_confidence, case_reason WHERE org_id=? AND id=?
systemEffects: inquiries row updated with case attribution metadata
rules.evidence: _create_case_for_inquiry: update inquiries SET case_source='ai', case_confidence=1.0, case_reason='automatisch: neuer Fall'; _attach: case_source='ai', case_confidence=round(sim,2), case_reason=f'automatisch zugeordnet (Ähnlichkeit {sim:.2f})'

rules.name: Project Progress Computed Field
rules.description: Both the list endpoint and the detail endpoint compute a 'progress' field as: round(done_appointments / total_appointments * 100) if total_appointments > 0, else 0. 'Done' means appointment.status == 'completed'.
rules.purpose: Gives staff a quick percentage-complete view of project work based on appointment completion rate.
actions: Compute progress = round(done/total*100) or 0
rules.evidence: p['progress'] = round(done / total * 100) if total else 0

rules.name: Invoice Budget Computation — Cancelled Excluded
rules.description: actual_budget (shown in the project detail and list) is the sum of invoice totals where status != 'cancelled'. open_amount is the sum of invoice totals where status == 'sent'. Cancelled invoices are excluded from both totals.
rules.purpose: Accurately reflects real financial exposure: cancelled invoices should not inflate the budget.
actions: actual = sum(v.total for v in invs if v.status != 'cancelled')
open_inv = sum(v.total for v in invs if v.status == 'sent')
rules.evidence: open_inv = round(sum((v.get('total') or 0) for v in invs if v.get('status') == 'sent'), 2); actual = round(sum((v.get('total') or 0) for v in invs if v.get('status') != 'cancelled'), 2)

rules.name: Project Form — Case Context Pre-fill and Auto-Link
rules.description: When ProjectFormPage is opened with URL params ?customer_id=&case_id=&case_number=, the customer field is pre-filled and locked (disabled for editing), the customer's address is auto-fetched and parsed into street/postcode/city fields. On save, after creating the project, the frontend immediately calls POST /api/projects/{new_id}/cases with the originating case_id to link the case to the new project. On success, the user is navigated back to the case (/cases?case={case_id}).
rules.purpose: Streamlines the 'create project from a case' workflow: customer and address are pre-populated, and the case is automatically attached.
validations: Title must be non-empty (UI enforced: save button disabled)
Customer must be selected (UI enforced: save button disabled)
actions: POST /api/projects with title, customer_id, and other fields
POST /api/projects/{new_id}/cases with {case_id: attachCaseId}
failureConditions: API error → 'Speichern fehlgeschlagen.' error message shown
rules.evidence: attachCaseId = !isEdit ? params.get('case_id') || '' : ''; if (attachCaseId && res.id) { await apiFetch(`/api/projects/${res.id}/cases`, { method: 'POST', body: JSON.stringify({ case_id: attachCaseId }) }) }

rules.name: Fälle Tab — Case Candidate Filter (Same Customer)
rules.description: In the ProjectWorkspacePage Fälle tab, the 'Fall hinzufügen' dropdown only shows cases not already in this project AND (if the project has a customer_id) belonging to the same customer. This mirrors the backend validation in PROJ-009 but is applied client-side using the cached /api/cases response.
rules.purpose: Prevents staff from accidentally attaching a case from a different customer to the project.
rules.trigger: User opens 'Fall hinzufügen' dropdown in Fälle tab
validations: Client-side: c.project_id !== project.id AND (!project.customer_id OR c.customer_id === project.customer_id)
rules.evidence: const candidates = cases.filter((c) => c.project_id !== project.id && (!project.customer_id || c.customer_id === project.customer_id))

rules.name: Project Required Fields — Title and Customer (UI Enforcement Only)
rules.description: In the ProjectFormPage, the save button is disabled if title is empty or customer is not selected. There is NO backend enforcement of title being non-null or customer_id being set on the POST schema (ProjectUpsert has title as required but customer_id is Optional). The backend accepts a project without customer_id.
rules.purpose: Guides staff to always associate a project with a customer, though the backend does not enforce this.
rules.evidence: UI: disabled={!title.trim() || !customerId || save.isPending}. Backend schema: title: str (required), customer_id: str | None = None (optional). Backend allows customer-less project creation.

rules.name: Activity Feed — Chronological Event Stream
rules.description: GET /api/projects/{id}/activity returns up to {limit} (default 20) events from the project's history, sourced from: new inquiries (type=inquiry), appointments (planned or completed), cost estimates created, invoices (sent/created), payments received, and inbound/outbound calls. All items are sorted by date descending, filtered to items with a non-null date field. Calls are fetched up to limit=10 from the member inquiries chain.
rules.purpose: Gives staff a unified timeline view of all meaningful events in a project.
actions: Resolve project → cases → inquiries → action rows
Build activity items from inquiries, appointments, KVAs, invoices, calls
Filter out items with no date
Sort by date desc
Slice to limit
outputs: List of {type, date, label, amount?} sorted chronologically
failureConditions: HTTP 404 if project not found
rules.evidence: items = [it for it in items if it.get('date')]; items.sort(key=lambda x: str(x['date']), reverse=True); return items[:limit]

rules.name: Org Disabled Gate — Project Access Blocked
rules.description: All project routes depend on require_org which checks if the caller's organization has a disabled_at timestamp set. If the org is disabled, all project API calls return HTTP 403 ('Diese Organisation ist deaktiviert.'). Super-admins bypass this check.
rules.purpose: Prevents access to project data when an organization has been suspended.
validations: If org.disabled_at is set and user.role != 'super_admin': HTTP 403
rules.evidence: require_org: if org_rows and org_rows[0].get('disabled_at'): raise HTTPException(status_code=HTTP_403_FORBIDDEN, detail='Diese Organisation ist deaktiviert.')

rules.name: Project Address — Structured JSON Field
rules.description: The project_address field is stored as a JSON object with keys {street, postcode, city}. It is populated from user input in the ProjectFormPage (either manually typed or auto-parsed from the customer's raw address). The backend accepts any dict without schema validation on the address shape. Can be set to null to clear.
rules.purpose: Allows a project to have a site address (e.g. a construction site) that differs from the customer's billing address.
validations: None server-side; UI validates hasAddr = street || postcode || city
actions: Store project_address as JSONB in projects table
rules.evidence: project_address: dict | None = None  # {street, postcode, city} in ProjectUpsert schema; UI: hasAddr = street || postcode || city; project_address: hasAddr ? { street, postcode, city } : null
